descriptions: Ghetto Brothers called a peace meeting attended by Magnificent Seven.
edges.evidence_quote: It took place on December 8, 1971, and was attended by two hundred representatives from dozens of Bronx gangs, including the Black Pearls, Black Spades, Dirty Dozens, Dutchmen, Latin Spades, Liberated Panthers, Magnificent Seven, Peacemakers, Royal Javelins, Savage Skulls, Seven Immortals, Turbans, and Young Sinners, together with police and city officials.

Bonde dos 40.description: Brazilian criminal organization founded in 2007 at the Pedrinhas Penitentiary Complex in São Luís, Maranhão. It operates in Maranhão, Piauí, and Mato Grosso, engaging in drug trafficking, murders, robberies, and prison rebellions. Originally allied with Primeiro Comando da Capital, it later broke with them and entered conflicts with Comando Vermelho. Considered one of Brazil's largest criminal gangs, it exerts influence in the Northeast region.

descriptions: Bonde dos 40 formed an alliance with Amigos dos Amigos.
edges.evidence_quote: they began operating both inside and outside prisons in the interior of Maranhão, even forming alliances with Comando Vermelho (CV) and Amigos dos Amigos (ADA).

descriptions: B40 is allied with ADA.
edges.evidence_quote: aliada à facção carioca ADA

descriptions: Infobox lists Bonde do Maluco as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Guardiões do Estado , Terceiro Comando Puro , Nova Okaida, Sindicato do Crime do Rio Grande do Norte, Bonde do Maluco , Comando Classe A

edges.evidence_quote: Until 2017, it was an ally of the Primeiro Comando da Capital , but they broke with their former allies in Teresina , sparking an intense war.

descriptions: Bonde dos 40 has been violently battling PCM for control.
edges.evidence_quote: the group became known for instigating riots in prisons and in the Pedrinhas Penitentiary Complex against the Primeiro Comando do Maranhão (PCM), and for carrying out attacks on the streets, resulting in the deaths of over 100 inmates. Since then, PCM and B40 have been violently battling for control over stilt neighborhoods and peripheral communities, causing dozens of deaths and injuries.

French Connection.description: The French Connection was a heroin smuggling scheme that operated from the 1930s to the 1970s, trafficking heroin from Indochina through Turkey to France and then to the United States and Canada. It was headed by Corsican gangsters Antoine Guérini and Paul Carbone, and supplied the majority of heroin in the US during its peak in the 1960s. The operation was dismantled in the 1970s.

descriptions: François Spirito was a leader of the French Connection, indicating the clan's involvement.
edges.evidence_quote: He was one of the leaders of the French Connection

descriptions: The Clan dei marsigliesi originated from members of the French Connection milieu.
edges.evidence_quote: provenienti in gran parte dal cosiddetto milieu marsigliese , il cartello criminale francese meglio conosciuto come " French Connection "

descriptions: The French Connection used funds stolen by the Carlingue to set up drug laboratories.
edges.evidence_quote: An agent of Henri Lafont , a member of the Carlingue (French auxiliaries of the Gestapo ), under the Vichy regime , he used part of the funds stolen by the Carlingue during the war to create drug laboratories near Marseille .

descriptions: Carbone and Spirito, leaders of the French Connection, joined the Carlingue during World War II.
edges.evidence_quote: During World War II, Carbone and Spirito joined the Carlingue, which collaborated with the Germans in France; in return, the local civilian authorities in Marseille were expected to ignore their criminal activities.

descriptions: The Amirante clan was allied with the Ferraiuolo-Stolder clan.
edges.evidence_quote: quest'ultimi alleati del clan Ferraiuolo-Stolder

descriptions: Ferraiuolo-Stolder was allied with Amirante, who opposed Mazzarella.
edges.evidence_quote: quest'ultimi alleati del clan Ferraiuolo-Stolder

descriptions: Ferraiuolo-Stolder is a branch (costola) of Clan Mazzarella.
edges.evidence_quote: Quest'ultimo asserente al clan Stolder-Ferraiuolo (costola dei Mazzarella).

descriptions: The American Mafia has done business with Chinese Tongs.
edges.evidence_quote: has at various times done business with the Irish Mob , Jewish-American organized crime , the Japanese yakuza , Indian mafia , the Russian mafia , Thief in law and Post-Soviet Organized crime groups, the Chinese triads , Chinese Tongs and Asian street gangs, Motorcycle Gangs and numerous White, Black and Hispanic prison and street gangs.

descriptions: East Coast Crips and Hoover Crips severed their alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The East Coast Crips (from East Los Angeles) and the Hoover Crips directly severed their alliance after Washington's death.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bronx Boys , True Kings

Volksfront.description: American white separatist organization founded in 1994 by Randal Lee Krager and Richard Arden while incarcerated in Oregon. It aimed to create an all-white homeland in the Pacific Northwest and had chapters in the US and several European countries. The group was considered neo-Nazi and a racist skinhead group, with about 50 members in the US. It dissolved in August 2012.

descriptions: Members of Volksfront were linked to Blood & Honour.
edges.evidence_quote: A number of Volksfront's members were linked with the Hammerskins, a white supremacist group formed in Texas, and Blood & Honour, a group founded in the United Kingdom in 1987.

descriptions: Members of Volksfront were linked to Hammerskins.
edges.evidence_quote: A number of Volksfront's members were linked with the Hammerskins, a white supremacist group formed in Texas

descriptions: Both organizations use the same neo-Nazi symbol, but no direct relationship is asserted.
edges.evidence_quote: The logo of Volksfront was the Algiz rune, a common rune used as a neo-Nazi symbol common among other organizations such as National Alliance.

descriptions: Northern Order is an affiliated organization of Atomwaffen Division in Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: The group also had a presence in Canada via an affiliated organization called Northern Order that included members of the Canadian Armed Forces.

Black Flag Army.description: The Black Flag Army was a bandit and mercenary group recruited largely from ethnic Zhuang and former Taiping soldiers, active in northern Vietnam from the 1860s to 1885. Led by Liu Yongfu, they fought against French colonial forces and other bandits, operating under Vietnamese and Chinese sponsorship. The army was officially disbanded in 1885 due to the Treaty of Tientsin, but remnants continued guerrilla warfare.

descriptions: The Black Flag Army was formed from former Taiping soldiers.
edges.evidence_quote: was a splinter remnant of a bandit and mercenary group recruited largely from soldiers of ethnic Zhuang background and former Taiping soldiers

descriptions: The Black Flag Army fought against the White Flag Army.
edges.evidence_quote: the Black Flag Army also successfully fought other groups of bandits and mercenaries, such as the White Flag Army and Yellow Flag Army.

descriptions: The Yellow Flags were subsidized to check the Black Flags, indicating a conflict relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: The Anamese subsidized the Yellow Flags partly to act as a check upon the troublesome Black Flags

edges.evidence_quote: Main groups within the AFCA hooligan firm are: - AFCA Maluku

Chechen mafia.description: The Chechen mafia is a large ethnic organized crime group operating primarily in the former Soviet Union, founded in the 1970s by Nikolay Suleimanov. It is known for its use of tribal teip structure and the abrek concept, and has been involved in money laundering, racketeering, drug trafficking, and other crimes. The group became dominant in Moscow during the 1990s and has ties to Chechen separatist movements.

descriptions: Albanian mafia colludes with Chechen mafia in Germany.
edges.evidence_quote: The Albanian mafia is involved in smuggling heroin and cocaine into Germany in collusion with Italian, Armenian and Chechen mafia groups.

descriptions: The Azerbaijani mafia is allied with the Chechen mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Turkish mafia , Pakistani mafia , Russian mafia , Chechen mafia

descriptions: The Chechen mafia has the Lebanese mafia as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Lebanese mafia , Russian mafia , Ukrainian mafia , Serbian mafia

descriptions: The Chechen mafia was in conflict with the Orekhovskaya gang.
edges.evidence_quote: This brought them into conflict with the Slavic gangs, including the Solntsevskaya bratva and the Orekhovskaya gang

descriptions: The Chechen mafia operates alongside the Russian mafia in the same region.
edges.evidence_quote: The Chechen mafia ... is one of the largest ethnic organized crime groups operating in the former Soviet Union next to established Russian mafia groups.

descriptions: The Chechen mafia has the Serbian mafia as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Lebanese mafia , Russian mafia , Ukrainian mafia , Serbian mafia

descriptions: Chechen mafia limits Slovak mafia activities.
edges.evidence_quote: their activities are limited by boundaries set by the powerful Russian mafia , Ukrainian mafia , Chechen mafia , and various Balkan groups

descriptions: The Chechen mafia was in conflict with the Solntsevskaya bratva.

descriptions: Chechen mafia challenged Solntsevskaya's dominance, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: But by the early 1990s, the Solntsevo's dominance was challenged by the Chechen mafia.

Market Street Commandos.description: The Market Street Commandos was an outlaw motorcycle club that participated in the 1947 Hollister incident, later immortalized in the film The Wild One. In 1954, the club merged with the Hells Angels to become their San Francisco chapter. The club is known for its role in early outlaw biker history.

descriptions: San Francisco Hells Angels charter founded by former Market Street Commandos members.
edges.evidence_quote: The San Francisco ("Frisco") charter was reportedly founded by former members of the Market Street Commandos in 1954.

descriptions: Both clubs participated together in the 1947 Hollister incident.
edges.evidence_quote: The Market Street Commandos was an outlaw motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington , participated in the highly publicized Hollister incident

descriptions: Market Street Commandos merged with Hells Angels to become their San Francisco chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1954 the Market Street Commandos merged with the Hells Angels to become their San Francisco chapter.

descriptions: Both clubs participated together in the 1947 Hollister incident.
edges.evidence_quote: The Market Street Commandos was an outlaw motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington , participated in the highly publicized Hollister incident

descriptions: POBOB and Market Street Commandos jointly participated in the Hollister riot in 1947.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington (POBOB) is a motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Market Street Commandos , participated in the highly publicized Hollister riot

descriptions: Cherry Hill Gambinos is a faction (sub-unit) of the Gambino family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sicilian faction of the Gambino family, known as the Cherry Hill Gambinos, is a group of Sicilian mafiosi operating as an arm of the family

descriptions: The Croxteth Crew killed a member of the Norris Green Strand Crew, indicating a violent rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The murder came the day before the first anniversary of the killing of Liam Smith, an alleged member of a rival gang, the Norris Green Strand Crew, who was shot dead by members of the Croxteth Crew on 23 August 2006.

descriptions: The two gangs were engaged in a violent feud involving shootings and murders.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2007, the feud between two street gangs from Croxteth and Norris Green reached its peak with the murder of innocent 11 year old Rhys Jones .

descriptions: The Crocky Crew and Strand Gang were engaged in a violent conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: the ongoing war between two rival gangs the ‘Crocky Crew’ and ‘Strand Gang’

edges.evidence_quote: he and his brother Dino, also present, were protected by a multitude of bikers from the Crazy Pistons Motorcycle Club in Brooklyn, who, along with the Cammaranos, got into a confrontation with the other mobsters.

descriptions: Untouchable Bishops were bitter enemies of South Brooklyn Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Also bitter enemies of SBB were the Puerto Rican Gangs, The Untouchable Bishops, and the Apaches, and the African American gangs The Mau Maus, and the Chaplains.

F-side.description: Dutch football hooligan group associated with AFC Ajax, founded in 1976 in Amsterdam. Named after the Vak F stand in Ajax's former stadium De Meer. Known for violent clashes with rival hooligan groups, including the Battle of Beverwijk in 1997 where a member was killed. Still active, with a presence in the Amsterdam Arena.

descriptions: F-side is a predecessor firm to AFCA.
edges.evidence_quote: In the past the Ajax fans consists only out of one firm: the well known F-side.
